title: Accountants and auditors
rationale: Accounting and auditing are performed entirely through computer systems. No physical tasks are part of the core work.

title: Actors
rationale: Acting involves physical performance and emotional expression. Conveying authentic human emotion is beyond current robot capabilities.

title: Actuaries
rationale: Actuarial work involves statistical modeling and risk analysis entirely through computer software. No physical tasks.

title: Administrative services and facilities managers
rationale: Involves physical inspections of buildings. Professional judgment about facility conditions requires human expertise.

title: Adult basic and secondary education and ESL teachers
rationale: Teaching involves instruction and individualized support requiring adaptive interpersonal communication. Not physical labor.

title: Advertising, promotions, and marketing managers
rationale: Marketing management involves strategy development, data analysis, and content creation on computers. No physical tasks a robot could perform.

title: Advertising sales agents
rationale: Involves prospecting clients and presenting media packages. No physical tasks required.

title: Aerospace engineering and operations technologists and technicians
rationale: Build test facilities and install instruments in structured aerospace environments. Precision and safety limit displacement.

title: Aerospace engineers
rationale: Predominantly digital—simulation and CAD design. Physical testing requires specialized expert judgment.

title: Agricultural and food science technicians
rationale: Structured laboratory procedures and some field sampling. Controlled environments are more amenable to robots.

title: Agricultural and food scientists
rationale: Split between field sampling in outdoor environments and laboratory analysis. Lab tasks follow more structured protocols.

title: Agricultural engineers
rationale: Combines digital design with physical field testing. Field conditions vary significantly with seasons and weather.

title: Agricultural workers
rationale: Physical labor in outdoor environments with variability in crop type and terrain. Specialized robots exist for specific crops.

title: Air traffic controllers
rationale: Involves monitoring radar screens and issuing voice instructions—entirely a cognitive and communicative function.

title: Aircraft and avionics equipment mechanics and technicians
rationale: Precise inspection and repair under strict FAA regulations. Safety-critical nature and diversity of aircraft create barriers.

title: Airline and commercial pilots
rationale: Advanced autopilot handles routine flight, but emergency management and sensory judgment in adverse conditions require human pilots.

title: Animal care and service workers
rationale: Physical handling of diverse species with unpredictable behavior. Human-animal bond is central to effective care.

title: Announcers and DJs
rationale: Broadcasting involves operating audio equipment and delivering content. Live DJ work requires real-time crowd engagement robots cannot replicate.

title: Anthropologists and archeologists
rationale: Physical excavation in varied field sites. Expert judgment for artifact identification is beyond robot capabilities.

title: Arbitrators, mediators, and conciliators
rationale: Dispute resolution requires active listening and facilitation of human dialogue. No physical tasks.

title: Architects
rationale: Architecture is predominantly digital—CAD and BIM modeling. Site visits require professional judgment, not physical labor.

title: Architectural and engineering managers
rationale: This managerial role involves project planning and team leadership through digital tools. No physical labor required.

title: Archivists, curators, and museum workers
rationale: Includes physical handling of delicate objects and cataloging in structured storage environments. Requires extreme care for irreplaceable artifacts.

title: Art directors
rationale: Art direction involves creative decision-making and digital design review. No physical manipulation of materials is required for core duties.

title: Assemblers and fabricators
rationale: Repetitive, standardized physical tasks in structured factory environments. Among the most amenable to humanoid robot replacement.

title: Athletes and sports competitors
rationale: The human body IS the product. No automated system can replace human athletic competition that fans pay to watch.

title: Athletic trainers
rationale: Physical taping, bracing, and emergency response on sports fields. Dynamic environments require human clinical judgment.

title: Atmospheric scientists, including meteorologists
rationale: Predominantly computer-based involving data analysis and model runs. Some fieldwork involves deploying sensors in varied conditions.

title: Audiologists
rationale: Physical clinical procedures requiring precision patient interaction. Interpersonal counseling limits displacement.

title: Automotive body and glass repairers
rationale: Highly variable damage patterns requiring creative problem-solving. Structured paint booth operations have more robot potential.

title: Automotive service technicians and mechanics
rationale: Diverse vehicle makes and failure modes create complex, unstructured physical challenges for robots.

title: Bakers
rationale: Repetitive physical tasks in structured bakery environments. Industrial baking is already automated; custom decorating is harder.

title: Barbers, hairstylists, and cosmetologists
rationale: Extraordinary tactile precision on diverse hair types. Social and experiential nature limits displacement.

title: Bartenders
rationale: Structured bar environments with repetitive drink preparation. Social hospitality limits full displacement.

title: Bill and account collectors
rationale: The core of this occupation—phone calls, skip tracing, and computer-based data analysis—is entirely digital. Occasional field visits represent a small minority of the work.

title: Biochemists and biophysicists
rationale: Split between digital research and physical laboratory work. Lab environments are structured but experiments involve complex biological systems.

title: Bioengineers and biomedical engineers
rationale: Involves digital design combined with physical lab and clinical equipment work in diverse contexts.

title: Biological technicians
rationale: Structured lab tasks—pipetting, sample preparation—follow standardized protocols. Recognizing anomalies requires human expertise.

title: Boilermakers
rationale: Fabricating and installing pressure vessels in confined spaces. Heavy welding and diverse site conditions limit displacement.

title: Bookkeeping, accounting, and auditing clerks
rationale: All core tasks—data entry, ledger reconciliation, and financial record-keeping—are performed entirely on a computer. Physical presence is unnecessary for the core functions.

title: Broadcast, sound, and video technicians
rationale: Involves physical equipment setup—rigging lights, running cables—in structured studio environments. Repetitive tasks have robot applicability.

title: Budget analysts
rationale: Budget analysis is entirely computer-based involving financial modeling, cost-benefit analysis, and report writing.

title: Bus drivers
rationale: Fixed-route driving is automatable. Managing passenger behavior and assisting disabled persons require human presence.

title: Butchers
rationale: Repetitive cutting in structured cold-processing environments. Industrial processing is already automated; retail custom cuts are harder.

title: Calibration technologists and technicians
rationale: Adjust instruments in structured environments following defined procedures. Diverse instrument types moderate displacement.

title: Cardiovascular technologists and technicians
rationale: Position patients and attach electrodes in structured clinical environments. Patient variability and emergency response limit displacement.

title: Career and technical education teachers
rationale: Supervise hands-on training in workshops requiring physical demonstration and safety intervention.

title: Carpenters
rationale: Diverse physical tasks in highly variable construction environments. Structured manufacturing has more robot potential.

title: Cartographers and photogrammetrists
rationale: Majority involves processing satellite imagery and GIS data on computers. Some field data collection occurs in varied outdoor environments.

title: Cashiers
rationale: Cashier work occurs in structured retail environments with standardized workflows—scanning items and processing payments at fixed stations amenable to robot operation.

title: Chefs and head cooks
rationale: Combines creative culinary judgment with physical kitchen management. Sensory capabilities beyond current robots.

title: Chemical engineers
rationale: Involves process design and monitoring in industrial plant environments with hazardous materials requiring safety knowledge.

title: Chemical technicians
rationale: Perform standardized laboratory procedures in structured environments. Many protocols are suitable for robotic automation.

title: Chemists and materials scientists
rationale: Split between digital research and physical laboratory work. Experiments involve complex judgment and hazardous materials.

title: Childcare workers
rationale: Constant physical supervision and safety management for young children. Nurturing human presence is fundamental.

title: Chiropractors
rationale: Precise manual adjustments requiring tactile assessment and real-time adaptation. Patient trust is essential.

title: Civil engineering technologists and technicians
rationale: Perform field surveys, material testing, and inspections in diverse outdoor environments. Some structured testing could be assisted.

title: Civil engineers
rationale: Involves digital design and analysis combined with physical site inspections in complex, hazardous environments.

title: Claims adjusters, appraisers, examiners, and investigators
rationale: Primarily digital document review. Field investigators visit sites in varied, unstructured environments requiring professional judgment.

title: Clinical laboratory technologists and technicians
rationale: Structured lab environments with defined protocols create meaningful robot applicability. Diversity of samples moderates risk.

title: Coaches and scouts
rationale: Physical instruction and motivational leadership in dynamic sports environments. Robots cannot provide athletic credibility.

title: Community health workers
rationale: Health education and support in diverse community settings. Human cultural connection is the primary value.

title: Compensation and benefits managers
rationale: Involves data analysis and strategic HR planning through digital tools. No physical tasks.

title: Compensation, benefits, and job analysis specialists
rationale: This role involves data analysis, benchmarking compensation, and drafting job descriptions entirely through computer systems.

title: Compliance officers
rationale: Compliance work involves analyzing regulatory documents and auditing data on computer systems. No physical tasks are part of the core function.

title: Computer and information research scientists
rationale: Research scientists in computing work at computers writing algorithms and analyzing experimental results. No manual labor is required.

title: Computer and information systems managers
rationale: IT management involves strategic planning and technical oversight conducted digitally and through meetings. No physical tasks.

title: Computer hardware engineers
rationale: Hardware engineering involves digital design work but also physical prototyping and circuit testing in structured lab environments.

title: Computer network architects
rationale: Network architecture design is primarily digital. Occasional physical hardware deployment represents a small fraction of the role.

title: Computer programmers
rationale: Computer programming is performed entirely on computers through writing, testing, and debugging code. No physical presence is required.

title: Computer support specialists
rationale: Many tasks are digital, but physical hardware setup, cable management, and on-site equipment replacement represent a meaningful portion in structured server rooms.

title: Computer systems analysts
rationale: Systems analysis involves designing and documenting technical systems entirely through digital tools. No physical manipulation required.

title: Concierges
rationale: Work at structured hotel lobbies. Basic tasks could be automated but luxury hospitality requires human warmth.

title: Conservation scientists and foresters
rationale: Physically demanding outdoor work—fire suppression, planting—in unpredictable natural environments.

title: Construction and building inspectors
rationale: Navigate diverse building environments performing physical inspections. Sites include tight spaces and hazardous conditions.

title: Construction equipment operators
rationale: Heavy equipment operation in dynamic outdoor sites. Variable terrain and real-time coordination create challenges.

title: Construction laborers and helpers
rationale: Carrying materials in variable outdoor construction environments. Active sites are among the most unstructured for robots.

title: Construction managers
rationale: Involves physical site oversight in complex, hazardous construction environments. Requires professional judgment and authority.

title: Cooks
rationale: Institutional cooking is repetitive in structured kitchens. Fine dining requires creative judgment and sensory assessment.

title: Correctional officers and bailiffs
rationale: Unpredictable and potentially violent institutional environments. Human authority and social intelligence are essential.

title: Cost estimators
rationale: Core cost estimation involves analyzing blueprints on computers. Occasional site visits represent a small portion of the work.

title: Court reporters and simultaneous captioners
rationale: Court reporters operate stenographic machines in controlled courtroom environments. The physical footprint is minimal, and speech recognition software is the relevant competitor.

title: Craft and fine artists
rationale: Involves physical manipulation of diverse materials requiring highly refined tactile judgment and creative expression.

title: Credit counselors
rationale: Involves analyzing financial data and providing personalized guidance through interpersonal sessions. No physical tasks.

title: Customer service representatives
rationale: The vast majority of this role is performed via phone, computer, or chat interface with no physical component. A humanoid robot adds negligible value over existing software for digital communication tasks.

title: Dancers and choreographers
rationale: Dance is the human body as artistic instrument. Robots lack fluid, expressive movement and emotional authenticity.

title: Data scientists
rationale: Data science is a purely digital occupation involving coding, statistical modeling, and data pipeline work. The entire value chain exists in software and data.

title: Database administrators and architects
rationale: Database work involves writing SQL, designing schemas, and monitoring system performance—all on computers. Physical presence is not required.

title: Delivery truck drivers and driver/sales workers
rationale: Driving is increasingly automatable. Physical last-mile delivery in diverse environments remains challenging.

title: Dental and ophthalmic laboratory technicians and medical appliance technicians
rationale: Fabricate dental prosthetics using structured manufacturing processes. Repetitive tasks have robot applicability; custom fitting moderates it.

title: Dental assistants
rationale: Physical clinical tasks in structured dental operatories. Fine motor coordination and patient communication limit displacement.

title: Dental hygienists
rationale: Highly precise manual procedures in the confined oral cavity. Safety risks and patient management limit displacement.

title: Dentists
rationale: Extraordinarily precise manual procedures in confined oral cavity. Sub-millimeter accuracy and patient variability create barriers.

title: Desktop publishers
rationale: Desktop publishing involves layout design and formatting using computer software. All work is digital, requiring no physical presence.

title: Diagnostic medical sonographers
rationale: Requires precise transducer manipulation with real-time image quality adjustment. Structured clinical environment offers some applicability.

title: Diesel service technicians and mechanics
rationale: Work on large, heavy vehicles with enormous variety of systems and failure modes.

title: Dietitians and nutritionists
rationale: Involves nutrition assessment and counseling through clinical consultations. No meaningful physical tasks.

title: Drafters
rationale: Drafters convert engineering concepts into CAD and BIM models using specialized computer software. All core work is digital.

title: Drywall installers, ceiling tile installers, and tapers
rationale: Repetitive indoor tasks—hanging sheets, mudding, taping. Ceiling work at height and finishing requiring aesthetic judgment moderate displacement.

title: Economists
rationale: Economists conduct research, analyze data, and write reports entirely through digital tools. No physical labor is involved.

title: Editors
rationale: Editing involves reviewing and revising text using computers, requiring no physical labor. All core tasks exist entirely in the digital domain.

title: Electrical and electronic engineering technologists and technicians
rationale: Perform soldering, assembly, and testing in structured lab environments. Fine precision in electronics limits broader displacement.

title: Electrical and electronics engineers
rationale: Primarily digital design and documentation. Physical lab testing requires specialized judgment.

title: Electrical and electronics installers and repairers
rationale: Diverse environments and fine motor precision for delicate components create challenges for robots.

title: Electrical power-line installers and repairers
rationale: Extreme physical demands at heights in all weather conditions. Safety-critical high-voltage work creates major barriers.

title: Electricians
rationale: Complex, unstructured environments—crawl spaces, attics, construction sites. Extreme diversity of systems requires adaptive intelligence.

title: Electro-mechanical and mechatronics technologists and technicians
rationale: Assembly, testing, and repair of robotic systems in structured environments. Complexity of troubleshooting limits displacement.

title: Elementary, middle, and high school principals
rationale: Requires physical presence for safety oversight but involves leadership and relationship management, not physical labor.

title: Elevator and escalator installers and repairers
rationale: Confined elevator shafts with precision mechanical work. Safety-critical systems and extreme height limit displacement.

title: Emergency management directors
rationale: Requires high-stakes real-time decision-making during disasters. No physical labor in the directorial function.

title: EMTs and paramedics
rationale: Physical intervention in unpredictable, chaotic environments. Fine clinical judgment and extreme variability limit robot deployment.

title: Entertainment and recreation managers
rationale: Involves physical facility oversight and management of dynamic recreational environments.

title: Environmental engineering technologists and technicians
rationale: Collect field samples and conduct testing in varied environments. Some structured procedures could be robot-assisted.

title: Environmental engineers
rationale: Combines digital system design with physical field inspections in varied, often challenging outdoor conditions.

title: Environmental science and protection technicians
rationale: Collect samples in varied environments. Lab testing follows more structured protocols.

title: Environmental scientists and specialists
rationale: Combines digital data analysis with fieldwork—collecting samples in varied natural environments.

title: Epidemiologists
rationale: Predominantly data-intensive digital work. Field investigations require interpersonal skills and professional judgment.

title: Exercise physiologists
rationale: Conduct physical stress tests requiring real-time patient observation. Clinical assessment limits robot applicability.

title: Farmers, ranchers, and other agricultural managers
rationale: Farm management in highly variable outdoor environments with biological and weather unpredictability.

title: Fashion designers
rationale: Involves digital CAD sketching combined with physical fabric handling and prototype construction requiring tactile judgment.

title: Film and video editors and camera operators
rationale: Editors work in digital environments. Camera operators manage physical equipment in diverse locations with creative judgment.

title: Financial analysts
rationale: Financial analysts work entirely in digital environments, processing datasets and generating reports on computers. No physical tasks exist that a humanoid robot could automate.

title: Financial clerks
rationale: Financial clerks process records, verify data, and manage payroll entirely through computer systems. No physical manipulation of objects is required.

title: Financial examiners
rationale: Financial examination involves reviewing documents entirely on computers. On-site audits require professional judgment, not physical labor.

title: Financial managers
rationale: Financial management involves data analysis, reporting, and advisory work performed on computers. No physical labor.

title: Fire inspectors
rationale: Navigate diverse building environments performing physical checks. Expert judgment for code violations limits robot applicability.

title: Firefighters
rationale: Physical operation in extreme, unpredictable hazardous environments. Life-threatening conditions far exceed robot capabilities.

title: Fishing and hunting workers
rationale: Most unpredictable natural environments—open ocean, dense forests. Environmental variability makes these least amenable to robots.

title: Fitness trainers and instructors
rationale: Physical demonstration and motivational coaching requiring human athletic performance and social connection.

title: Flight attendants
rationale: Safety management and passenger service in confined aircraft cabins. Emergency response and regulatory requirements limit displacement.

title: Flooring installers and tile and stone setters
rationale: Repetitive indoor installation with defined patterns. Custom tile work and irregular rooms moderate displacement.

title: Floral designers
rationale: Physical manipulation of fresh plant materials in workshop environments. Irregular living materials and creative design limit displacement.

title: Food and beverage serving and related workers
rationale: Structured dining environments with repetitive service tasks. Social hospitality experience limits full displacement.

title: Food preparation workers
rationale: Repetitive tasks in structured commercial kitchens. Controlled environments are moderately amenable to robot replacement.

title: Food processing equipment workers
rationale: Highly repetitive physical tasks in structured factory environments with controlled conditions. Among the more amenable to robot replacement.

title: Food service managers
rationale: Requires physical kitchen oversight and real-time staff management. Interpersonal leadership cannot be replicated.

title: Forensic science technicians
rationale: Collect evidence at unpredictable crime scenes and analyze samples in structured labs.

title: Forest and conservation workers
rationale: Rugged outdoor environments with extreme terrain variability. Emergency fire suppression is particularly challenging.

title: Fundraisers
rationale: Fundraising involves donor research and relationship cultivation. The core value lies in human trust and persuasion.

title: Funeral service workers
rationale: Physical preparation in structured mortuary environments. Grief counseling requires profound human empathy.

title: Gambling services workers
rationale: Dealers perform structured tasks at fixed gaming tables. Social entertainment atmosphere limits displacement.

title: General maintenance and repair workers
rationale: Repair diverse building systems in highly varied environments. No two jobs are identical, requiring adaptive problem-solving.

title: General office clerks
rationale: Core duties such as data entry, document formatting, and scheduling are entirely computer-based. A humanoid robot could theoretically handle physical mail sorting, but these tasks represent a small fraction of the role.

title: Genetic counselors
rationale: Involves interpreting DNA data and providing emotionally sensitive guidance. No physical tasks.

title: Geographers
rationale: Geography work involves analyzing GIS data on computers. Occasional fieldwork requires human navigation in unstructured outdoor settings.

title: Geological and hydrologic technicians
rationale: Conduct fieldwork in diverse outdoor environments. Unpredictable terrain limits robot applicability.

title: Geoscientists
rationale: Combine digital analysis with physical fieldwork in varied outdoor environments.

title: Glaziers
rationale: Diverse glass installations requiring strength and precision with fragile materials.

title: Graphic designers
rationale: Graphic design is performed entirely on computer workstations using digital design tools. No physical manipulation of materials is required.

title: Grounds maintenance workers
rationale: Repetitive outdoor tasks in semi-structured environments. Autonomous mowers exist; complex landscaping requires human judgment.

title: Hand laborers and material movers
rationale: Repetitive physical tasks in semi-structured warehouse environments. Controlled settings with standardized workflows are well-suited for robots.

title: Hazardous materials removal workers
rationale: Structured remediation procedures in contained work environments. Diverse building conditions moderate displacement.

title: Health and safety engineers
rationale: Involve physical inspection of industrial facilities. Professional judgment about hazards requires human expertise.

title: Health education specialists
rationale: Involves community outreach and interpersonal health coaching. No physical labor.

title: Health information technologists and medical registrars
rationale: This occupation involves classifying clinical data and managing electronic health records on computer systems—all digital tasks.

title: Heating, air conditioning, and refrigeration mechanics and installers
rationale: Complex climate systems in diverse building environments—attics, rooftops, tight spaces.

title: Heavy and tractor-trailer truck drivers
rationale: Highway driving is highly amenable to automation, but securing cargo and navigating complex loading yards remain challenging for robots.

title: Heavy vehicle and mobile equipment service technicians
rationale: Work on large equipment in diverse environments. Enormous variety creates complex physical challenges.

title: High school teachers
rationale: Teaching requires physical presence but the core value is cognitive and social—explaining concepts and managing student behavior. No physical labor.

title: Historians
rationale: Historical research involves archival research, document analysis, and writing—entirely cognitive and digital tasks.

title: Home health and personal care aides
rationale: Physical care in highly diverse residential environments. Home variability and intimate personal care require human empathy.

title: Human resources managers
rationale: HR management involves strategic people-management and policy oversight. No physical tasks required.

title: Human resources specialists
rationale: HR work involves recruiting, employee relations, and record-keeping through digital systems and interpersonal interactions. No physical labor.

title: Hydrologists
rationale: Combines digital data modeling with field sampling in varied natural water environments.

title: Industrial designers
rationale: Most work is performed through digital CAD and 3D modeling. Physical prototyping represents a small, highly skilled fraction.

title: Industrial engineering technologists and technicians
rationale: Work on manufacturing floors performing time-motion studies. Structured factory environments offer some robot applicability.

title: Industrial engineers
rationale: Predominantly digital—analyzing workflows and modeling systems on computers. Occasional shop floor visits require observation, not labor.

title: Industrial machinery mechanics, machinery maintenance workers, and millwrights
rationale: Factory environments are structured but equipment variety and failure modes require adaptive problem-solving.

title: Industrial production managers
rationale: Involves physical oversight of manufacturing facilities combined with digital planning. Factory supervision requires expert judgment.

title: Information clerks
rationale: Mostly computer-based data processing. Some roles at hotel desks or airports require physical presence and basic document handling.

title: Information security analysts
rationale: Information security is entirely digital—network monitoring, vulnerability analysis, and incident response through computer systems.

title: Instructional coordinators
rationale: Involves curriculum development and data analysis through digital tools and meetings. No physical tasks.

title: Insulation workers
rationale: Repetitive cutting and fitting in indoor construction environments. Confined spaces and varied conditions moderate displacement.

title: Insurance sales agents
rationale: Insurance sales involves analyzing policies and client communication through digital systems. In-person meetings require relationship-building, not labor.

title: Insurance underwriters
rationale: Underwriting involves analyzing financial documents and risk data entirely through computer systems. All value-generating work occurs in the digital domain.

title: Interior designers
rationale: Involves CAD modeling and client consultations. Site visits require aesthetic judgment, not physical labor.

title: Interpreters and translators
rationale: The vast majority of translation work is performed digitally. In-person interpretation involves no physical manipulation tasks a robot could perform.

title: Ironworkers
rationale: Physical work at extreme heights on structural steel frames in outdoor environments. Balance on narrow beams is far beyond robot capabilities.

title: Janitors and building cleaners
rationale: Highly repetitive physical tasks in semi-structured indoor environments. Autonomous cleaning robots exist; humanoid robots could extend to varied tasks.

title: Jewelers and precious stone and metal workers
rationale: Extraordinary fine motor precision at extreme limits of current robot dexterity, especially for custom work.

title: Judges and hearing officers
rationale: Judging requires human judgment and public accountability. No physical tasks.

title: Kindergarten and elementary school teachers
rationale: Requires constant physical presence with young children. Physical tasks include managing classroom activities and safety supervision.

title: Labor relations specialists
rationale: Involves contract drafting and interpersonal negotiation—all knowledge and communication-based work.

title: Landscape architects
rationale: Involves digital CADD modeling and site visits. Site visits require professional assessment, not physical labor.

title: Lawyers
rationale: Legal work is entirely knowledge-based—document drafting, legal research, and case strategy on computers. Physical courtroom presence involves only human advocacy.

title: Librarians and library media specialists
rationale: Librarians combine digital work with physical collection management. Community-facing functions require human judgment.

title: Library technicians and assistants
rationale: Library technicians perform repetitive physical tasks—reshelving books in structured environments. Well-suited for robot manipulation of standardized objects.

title: Licensed practical and licensed vocational nurses
rationale: Hands-on patient care in structured clinical settings. Patient complexity and safety-critical nature limit displacement.

title: Loan officers
rationale: Loan officers analyze financial documents through computer systems. Client meetings require interpersonal skills, not physical labor.

title: Lodging managers
rationale: Involves facility inspection and guest relations. Interpersonal warmth and adaptive management require human presence.

title: Logging workers
rationale: Extreme outdoor environments with highly variable terrain. Forest conditions are among the most challenging for robots.

title: Logisticians
rationale: Logistics management involves supply chain analysis and coordination through digital platforms. No physical labor.

title: Machinists and tool and die makers
rationale: Operate precision machine tools in structured environments. Custom tooling and blueprint interpretation moderate displacement.

title: Management analysts
rationale: Management consulting involves data analysis, report writing, and presentations—all through computers and meetings. No physical tasks.

title: Manicurists and pedicurists
rationale: Extreme tactile precision on small, variable nail surfaces. Social wellness experience limits displacement.

title: Marine engineers and naval architects
rationale: Involves digital design work combined with physical inspections of ships and offshore platforms in hazardous environments.

title: Market research analysts
rationale: All core work—data collection, statistical analysis, and report generation—is performed on computers. Physical presence is not required.

title: Marriage and family therapists
rationale: Relies on human empathy, interpersonal dynamics, and therapeutic relationship-building. No physical tasks.

title: Masonry workers
rationale: Outdoor construction environments with variable surfaces and weather. Repetitive brick-laying in controlled settings has more potential.

title: Massage therapists
rationale: Extraordinarily nuanced tactile pressure adjustment based on real-time tissue feedback. Therapeutic relationship is central.

title: Material moving machine operators
rationale: Repetitive material transport in structured warehouse environments. Defined routes and standardized pallets create strong applicability.

title: Material recording clerks
rationale: Clerks track inventory and scan items in warehouses—structured environments with repetitive physical tasks suitable for robot operation.

title: Materials engineers
rationale: Involves simulation and digital analysis combined with physical lab testing of material properties.

title: Mathematicians and statisticians
rationale: Mathematical and statistical work is entirely cognitive and computer-based, involving modeling, data analysis, and coding.

title: Mechanical engineering technologists and technicians
rationale: Perform assembly and testing in structured lab environments. Diversity of systems limits broader displacement.

title: Mechanical engineers
rationale: Predominantly digital—CAD modeling and simulation. Physical prototyping is specialized and non-repetitive.

title: Medical and health services managers
rationale: Healthcare management involves budgeting, staff oversight, and regulatory compliance. Physical presence is for supervision, not labor.

title: Medical assistants
rationale: Physical clinical tasks in structured settings. Patient variability and empathetic communication limit displacement.

title: Medical dosimetrists
rationale: Primarily digital treatment planning. Some physical tasks include calibrating radiation equipment in structured clinical environments.

title: Medical equipment repairers
rationale: Diverse hospital equipment in structured healthcare settings. Variety and precision of medical device repair limit displacement.

title: Medical records specialists
rationale: Medical records work involves classifying, coding, and entering clinical data into electronic health systems—all digital tasks.

title: Medical scientists
rationale: Split between digital research and physical laboratory work. Structured lab environments offer some robot applicability for routine tasks.

title: Medical transcriptionists
rationale: This occupation is entirely digital—workers transcribe audio into text using computers. A humanoid robot provides no physical advantage whatsoever for this task. No physical labor is involved at any stage of the work.

title: Meeting, convention, and event planners
rationale: Involves on-site event supervision requiring real-time problem-solving and interpersonal management.

title: Metal and plastic machine workers
rationale: Operate CNC equipment in structured factory environments with standardized, repetitive workflows well-suited for humanoid robots.

title: Microbiologists
rationale: Involves specialized laboratory work in controlled environments. Many protocols are standardized but require expert judgment.

title: Middle school teachers
rationale: Teaching involves classroom instruction and adolescent mentorship. Physical presence is for social authority, not labor.

title: Military careers
rationale: Vast range of physical activities in unpredictable, adversarial environments. Legal and ethical requirements limit robot replacement.

title: Mining and geological engineers
rationale: Involves digital mine planning with physical site evaluations in hazardous underground environments.

title: Models
rationale: Modeling involves physical posing in controlled studio environments. However, conveying authentic human emotion and aesthetic expression is beyond current robot capabilities.

title: Music directors and composers
rationale: Conducting requires nuanced human expression and real-time adaptive leadership. Composing occurs on digital notation software.

title: Musicians and singers
rationale: Live performance involves the human body as the primary artistic instrument. Robots lack expressive authenticity.

title: Natural sciences managers
rationale: Involves overseeing research programs and managing budgets. Lab presence is for supervision, not manual labor.

title: Network and computer systems administrators
rationale: Much is digital, but physical tasks like racking servers and running cables are genuine parts of the role in structured data centers.

title: News analysts, reporters, and journalists
rationale: Journalism involves field reporting and source cultivation—primarily cognitive and social activities.

title: Nuclear engineers
rationale: Involves digital design and simulation combined with physical facility inspections in radiation-hazardous environments.

title: Nuclear medicine technologists
rationale: Prepare radiopharmaceuticals and operate gamma cameras in structured environments. Radioactive material handling requires expertise.

title: Nuclear technicians
rationale: Work in structured plant environments. Controlled setting offers some applicability but radiation hazard and regulation limit deployment.

title: Nurse anesthetists, nurse midwives, and nurse practitioners
rationale: Complex clinical procedures require extraordinary precision and real-time adaptive judgment beyond robotic capability.

title: Nursing assistants and orderlies
rationale: Structured patient care tasks in hospital environments. Clinical setting offers more structure; patient variability moderates displacement.

title: Occupational health and safety specialists and technicians
rationale: Conduct physical worksite inspections in diverse industrial environments requiring professional judgment.

title: Occupational therapists
rationale: Physical assessment in diverse environments with hands-on therapeutic interventions. Individualized and patient-specific.

title: Occupational therapy assistants and aides
rationale: Guide patients through structured therapeutic exercises. Defined protocols offer some applicability; empathy limits displacement.

title: Oil and gas workers
rationale: Physically demanding work in hazardous, unpredictable environments. Extreme conditions create major barriers.

title: Operations research analysts
rationale: Operations research is entirely computer-based involving data modeling and optimization algorithms. Software and AI are the relevant automation forces.

title: Opticians
rationale: Physical fitting tasks in structured retail settings. Tactile adjustment on diverse faces moderates displacement.

title: Optometrists
rationale: Physical eye examinations and minor procedures requiring clinical precision. Patient counseling is essential.

title: Orthotists and prosthetists
rationale: Highly individualized physical measurement and custom fabrication requiring patient-specific adaptation.

title: Painters, construction and maintenance
rationale: Physical surface preparation and painting in diverse building environments. Structured industrial painting has more potential.

title: Painting and coating workers
rationale: Repetitive surface application in structured manufacturing environments. Industrial spray painting is already partially automated.

title: Paralegals and legal assistants
rationale: Core duties—legal research, document drafting, and case organization—are entirely digital. The automation risk is purely from AI language models, not physical robots.

title: Personal financial advisors
rationale: Financial advising is relationship-driven requiring trust, emotional reassurance, and complex ethical judgment. No physical tasks.

title: Pest control workers
rationale: Diverse and confined spaces—crawl spaces, attics—in unpredictable environments. Complex navigation required.

title: Petroleum engineers
rationale: Involves digital modeling combined with site visits to remote and offshore locations in hazardous environments.

title: Pharmacists
rationale: Retail dispensing involves repetitive structured tasks at fixed stations. Automated systems already exist. Clinical consultation remains human.

title: Pharmacy technicians
rationale: Highly structured, repetitive tasks at fixed dispensing stations. Automated dispensing robots already exist in hospitals.

title: Phlebotomists
rationale: Venipuncture in clinical settings requiring tactile assessment. Patient variability in vein accessibility limits displacement.

title: Photographers
rationale: Photography requires operating cameras in diverse environments with creative judgment. Robots could assist in controlled studios but not replace artistic decision-making.

title: Physical therapist assistants and aides
rationale: Structured exercise guidance in clinical settings. Defined protocols offer some applicability; tactile assessment limits displacement.

title: Physical therapists
rationale: Hands-on manual techniques requiring tactile precision and real-time patient response assessment.

title: Physician assistants
rationale: Perform physical examinations and clinical procedures requiring precision and patient-specific adaptation.

title: Physicians and surgeons
rationale: Medical procedures require extraordinary manual precision and real-time adaptation. Robotic surgical systems are tools, not autonomous replacements.

title: Physicists and astronomers
rationale: Predominantly digital work with data analysis and mathematical modeling. Physical lab tasks require specialized scientific expertise.

title: Plumbers, pipefitters, and steamfitters
rationale: Confined, variable environments with diverse pipe materials. Extreme variability in older buildings limits displacement.

title: Podiatrists
rationale: Precise clinical procedures requiring fine motor dexterity. Patient anatomy varies significantly.

title: Police and detectives
rationale: Physical presence in unpredictable community environments. Dynamic and high-stakes nature limits robot applicability.

title: Political scientists
rationale: Political science is entirely knowledge-based—research, data analysis, and writing on computers. No physical tasks required.

title: Postal service workers
rationale: Structured sorting environments and repetitive delivery routes create meaningful robot applicability.

title: Postsecondary education administrators
rationale: Higher education administration involves reviewing applications and managing budgets through digital systems. No physical labor.

title: Postsecondary teachers
rationale: Teaching involves lectures, discussions, and research—primarily cognitive and social activities. No physical labor required.

title: Power plant operators, distributors, and dispatchers
rationale: Operators monitor control panels and adjust equipment in structured industrial control rooms. Controlled environment with repetitive monitoring tasks.

title: Preschool and childcare center directors
rationale: Manage staff, oversee curriculum, and build family relationships. Physical presence is for oversight, not labor.

title: Preschool teachers
rationale: Constant physical supervision and care of young children. Emotional security from human caregivers is fundamental to child development.

title: Private detectives and investigators
rationale: Involves physical surveillance and in-person interviews in unpredictable environments. Requires inconspicuous human presence.

title: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists
rationale: Involves home visits and building relationships with resistant clients in unpredictable community settings.

title: Producers and directors
rationale: Producing involves creative decision-making and team leadership. Physical presence is for leadership and communication, not manual labor.

title: Project management specialists
rationale: Project management involves scheduling, documentation, and coordination through digital tools. No physical labor required.

title: Property appraisers and assessors
rationale: Involves physical site inspections combined with digital analysis. Inspections require professional valuation judgment.

title: Property, real estate, and community association managers
rationale: Involves physical inspections of diverse facilities combined with administrative and tenant relations work.

title: Psychiatric technicians and aides
rationale: Physical care and behavioral management for patients with serious mental illness. Unpredictable and potentially violent environments.

title: Psychologists
rationale: Requires empathy, clinical observation, and therapeutic relationship-building. No physical tasks.

title: Public relations and fundraising managers
rationale: PR management involves strategic planning and high-stakes interpersonal communication. No physical tasks.

title: Public relations specialists
rationale: PR work involves writing press releases and managing social media—all digital tasks. Event presence involves purely social interactions.

title: Public safety telecommunicators
rationale: Emergency dispatchers work at computer consoles processing voice communications. No physical tasks.

title: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents
rationale: Purchasing involves analyzing proposals and managing orders through computer systems. Occasional physical inspection is minor.

title: Quality control inspectors
rationale: Inspection at fixed stations in structured factories involves repetitive visual and tactile checks. Computer vision and robots can handle structured inspection tasks.

title: Radiation therapists
rationale: Position patients on treatment tables in structured rooms with defined protocols. Patient comfort and communication moderate displacement.

title: Radiologic and MRI technologists
rationale: Position patients and operate imaging equipment in structured clinical rooms. Patient communication limits displacement.

title: Railroad workers
rationale: Driving on fixed tracks is automatable. Physical trackside work in varied conditions moderates overall risk.

title: Real estate brokers and sales agents
rationale: Involves property showings and contract negotiation. Physical tours require navigation of diverse, unstructured spaces.

title: Receptionists
rationale: Receptionists work at fixed front desk stations. Physical tasks like accepting packages could be partially automated, but social gatekeeper function limits displacement.

title: Recreation workers
rationale: Supervise activities in dynamic community environments requiring adaptive human leadership.

title: Recreational therapists
rationale: Leading physical activities with patients requiring real-time assistance and emotional support.

title: Registered nurses
rationale: Complex physical tasks combined with interpersonal care. Clinical environment is somewhat structured but patient variability is enormous.

title: Rehabilitation counselors
rationale: Involves providing emotional support for people with disabilities. No physical tasks.

title: Respiratory therapists
rationale: Hands-on patient care in clinical settings. Emergency response and adaptive clinical judgment limit displacement.

title: Retail sales workers
rationale: Semi-structured retail environments with standardized shelving. Robots could stock shelves but social sales dynamics limit displacement.

title: Roofers
rationale: Extreme safety risks on sloped surfaces at heights in variable weather. Physical balance on slopes is beyond current robot capabilities.

title: Sales engineers
rationale: Combines technical knowledge with interpersonal persuasion. No physical labor in the core function.

title: Sales managers
rationale: Sales management involves CRM analysis, strategy development, and team leadership. No physical labor required.

title: School and career counselors and advisors
rationale: Involves providing emotional support, crisis intervention, and career guidance. No physical tasks.

title: Secretaries and administrative assistants
rationale: The vast majority involves scheduling, drafting correspondence, and managing databases on computers. Physical tasks like handling mail represent a small portion.

title: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents
rationale: Financial services sales is performed through digital trading platforms, phone calls, and computer-based analysis. No physical tasks.

title: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers
rationale: Structured patrol environments are suitable for robots. Physical detention and emergency response require human judgment.

title: Semiconductor processing technicians
rationale: Highly structured cleanroom environments with standardized equipment and defined protocols well-suited for robots.

title: Set and exhibit designers
rationale: Involves digital CAD work combined with physical oversight of set construction. Requires creative decision-making.

title: Sheet metal workers
rationale: Repetitive fabrication in structured shops has robot applicability. Field installation in construction environments is more variable.

title: Skincare specialists
rationale: Tactile precision for skin treatments with constant real-time adjustment. Interpersonal wellness experience limits displacement.

title: Small engine mechanics
rationale: Diverse engine types and failure modes in workshop environments. Routine maintenance has more robot potential.

title: Social and community service managers
rationale: Involves program oversight and stakeholder engagement requiring human empathy and leadership. No physical labor.

title: Social and human service assistants
rationale: Split between digital administrative work and physical home visits to support vulnerable clients.

title: Social workers
rationale: Navigate complex family environments, provide crisis intervention, and build trust. Robots cannot replicate this.

title: Sociologists
rationale: Involves research design, data analysis, and writing—predominantly digital. Fieldwork requires human social intelligence.

title: Software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers
rationale: This occupation is performed entirely at a computer; all tasks involve writing, testing, and reviewing code in a digital environment. A humanoid robot provides zero physical advantage.

title: Solar photovoltaic installers
rationale: Physical rooftop work in variable conditions. Safety risks and site diversity limit displacement.

title: Special education teachers
rationale: Requires highly individualized physical and emotional support for students with complex disabilities. Robots cannot replicate this.

title: Special effects artists and animators
rationale: Animation and VFX work is performed entirely on computer workstations using specialized software. No physical manipulation of materials is required.

title: Speech-language pathologists
rationale: Requires real-time physical observation and manual techniques for swallowing therapy with personalized coaching.

title: Stationary engineers and boiler operators
rationale: Structured mechanical plant environments with repetitive monitoring. Emergency response and confined space work require human expertise.

title: Substance abuse, behavioral disorder, and mental health counselors
rationale: Relies entirely on human therapeutic presence, empathy, and trust. No physical tasks.

title: Surgical assistants and technologists
rationale: Work in structured operating rooms with defined tasks. Dynamic surgical complications and fine motor demands limit displacement.

title: Survey researchers
rationale: Survey research is primarily digital—questionnaire design, data collection, and statistical analysis. Occasional fieldwork involves social intelligence robots cannot replicate.

title: Surveying and mapping technicians
rationale: Operate equipment in outdoor environments. Outdoor terrain variability limits robot applicability.

title: Surveyors
rationale: Navigating diverse outdoor terrain for precise measurements. Variable field conditions and legal certification limit displacement.

title: Tax examiners and collectors, and revenue agents
rationale: Bulk of work involves reviewing digital records. Field investigations require professional judgment rather than physical labor.

title: Taxi drivers, shuttle drivers, and chauffeurs
rationale: Driving is increasingly automatable, but assisting passengers with disabilities and handling luggage remain challenging.

title: Teacher assistants
rationale: Provide physical support to students requiring human empathy and behavioral response.

title: Technical writers
rationale: Technical writing involves researching, drafting, and editing documentation entirely on computers. No physical tasks required.

title: Telecommunications technicians
rationale: Climb towers and splice cables in diverse outdoor environments. Height work and confined spaces challenge robots.

title: Tellers
rationale: Tellers work at fixed counter stations performing standardized transactions—a structured environment. Customer service aspects complicate full displacement.

title: Top executives
rationale: Executive leadership involves strategic decision-making, organizational oversight, and interpersonal negotiation. No physical tasks.

title: Tour and travel guides
rationale: Lead groups through diverse environments requiring real-time adaptation. Robots cannot provide authentic cultural interpretation.

title: Training and development managers
rationale: Involves overseeing learning programs through digital tools. No physical tasks.

title: Training and development specialists
rationale: Training involves designing curricula and delivering instruction through digital platforms. Live delivery requires human adaptive communication.

title: Transportation, storage, and distribution managers
rationale: Involves overseeing logistics facilities and safety compliance. Physical presence is for oversight, not manual labor.

title: Travel agents
rationale: Travel agents work entirely on computers researching destinations, comparing prices, and booking itineraries. No physical interaction required.

title: Tutors
rationale: Tutoring is primarily cognitive and interpersonal. No physical labor is involved in the core function.

title: Umpires, referees, and other sports officials
rationale: Manage dynamic game environments. Computer vision can automate calls but managing conduct requires human authority.

title: Urban and regional planners
rationale: Involves GIS analysis and community engagement. Site visits require professional observation, not physical labor.

title: Veterinarians
rationale: Physical examination and surgery on animals with highly variable anatomy and temperament.

title: Veterinary assistants and laboratory animal caretakers
rationale: Handle diverse animal species with unpredictable behavior. Animal welfare and safety implications limit displacement.

title: Veterinary technologists and technicians
rationale: Physical clinical tasks with unpredictable animal behavior creating significant challenges for robots.

title: Waiters and waitresses
rationale: Structured restaurant environments with defined service procedures. Social hospitality limits displacement.

title: Water and wastewater treatment plant and system operators
rationale: Structured industrial environments with repetitive monitoring tasks. Emergency response requires human expertise.

title: Water transportation workers
rationale: Dynamic, unpredictable maritime environments. Extreme variability and safety-critical nature create barriers.

title: Web developers and digital designers
rationale: All tasks—writing HTML/CSS/JavaScript, designing layouts—are performed at a computer. This occupation is entirely within the digital domain.

title: Welders, cutters, solderers, and brazers
rationale: Industrial welding in structured settings involves repetitive standardized joints. Robotic welding already exists in factories.

title: Wholesale and manufacturing sales representatives
rationale: Sales work is primarily through digital communication. Product demonstrations involve social persuasion rather than physical labor.

title: Wind turbine technicians
rationale: Extreme height work, confined nacelle environments, and variable weather create major physical challenges.

title: Woodworkers
rationale: Repetitive tasks in structured workshops. CNC-driven work is already automated. Custom work has lower robot applicability.

title: Writers and authors
rationale: Writing and authorship is a purely cognitive and digital occupation. The automation threat comes entirely from language model AI, not robotics.

title: Zoologists and wildlife biologists
rationale: Physically demanding fieldwork in unpredictable natural settings. Interactions with wild animals require adaptive responses.
